conditions.notes: Certification must meet § 11087(a)(1); employer may not contact provider except to authenticate.
conditions.source_text: As a condition of granting a leave for the serious health condition of the employee's family member, the employer may require certification of the serious health condition.

conditions.notes: Employer may require second opinion at employer's expense if good-faith doubt; third opinion is binding.
conditions.source_text: As a condition of granting a leave for the serious health condition of the employee, the employer may require certification of the serious health condition.

conditions.notes: Employer must allow at least 15 days; leave may begin before cert is received.
conditions.source_text: The employer may require that the employee provide any certification within no less than 15 calendar days of the employer's request.

conditions.required_value: may require if additional leave requested
conditions.notes: Recertification only upon expiration of original estimated period and only if more leave is requested.
conditions.source_text: Upon expiration of the time period the health care provider originally estimated...the employer may require the employee to obtain recertification, but only if additional leave is requested.

conditions.notes: Release required only if employer has uniformly applied policy; not required for each intermittent absence unless safety concern (max once per 30 days).
conditions.source_text: As a condition of an employee's return from medical leave, the employer may require the employee to obtain a release to return-to-work...only if the employer has a uniformly applied practice or policy.

conditions.notes: Employer may use its own form; diagnosis disclosure requires patient consent.
conditions.source_text: The employer may utilize the following Certification of Health Care Provider form or its equivalent.

conditions.notes: Applies to reasonable accommodation, transfer, or pregnancy disability leave.
conditions.source_text: The employer may require medical certification of the medical advisability of the leave, as set forth in sections 11049(a) and (b), and 11050(b).

conditions.notes: Applies when leave is foreseeable and 30 days notice given; otherwise at least 15 calendar days after employer request.
conditions.source_text: When the leave is foreseeable and at least 30 days' notice has been provided, the employee shall provide the medical certification before the leave begins.

conditions.notes: Employer-set deadline must be at least 15 calendar days after request.
conditions.source_text: The employee shall provide the requested certification to the employer within the time frame requested by the employer (which must be at least 15 calendar days after the employer's request).

conditions.notes: Employer must request cert at time of notice or within 2 business days; for unforeseen leave, within 2 business days after leave commences.
conditions.source_text: The employer shall request that an employee furnish medical certification...at the time the employee gives notice...or within two business days thereafter.

conditions.notes: Required content for cert supporting reasonable accommodation or transfer.
conditions.source_text: The medical certification indicating the medical advisability of reasonable accommodation or a transfer is sufficient if it contains: a description, a statement of medical advisability, and the date and estimated duration.

conditions.notes: Required content for cert supporting pregnancy disability leave.
conditions.source_text: The medical certification indicating disability necessitating a leave is sufficient if it contains: a statement of disability and the date and estimated duration.

conditions.required_value: may require if additional time requested
conditions.notes: Recertification only upon expiration of original estimated period.
conditions.source_text: Upon expiration of the time period that the health care provider originally estimated the employee would need...the employer may require the employee to obtain recertification if additional time is requested.

conditions.notes: Only if employer has uniformly applied policy for similarly situated non-pregnancy disability employees.
conditions.source_text: The employer may require the employee to obtain a release to return-to-work...only if the employer has a uniformly applied practice or policy of requiring such releases from other similarly situated employees.

conditions.required_value: employer form, HCP form, or § 11050(e) form
conditions.notes: Employer may develop own form or use the Civil Rights Council form.
conditions.source_text: Employers requiring written medical certification...may develop their own form, utilize one provided by the employee's health care provider or use the form provided below.
